title: Epidemiological Analysis with pkmapr
text: Gilgit-Baltistan Azad Kashmir share land boundaries provinces. standard contiguity rules, zero neighbours — breaking spatial statistics. pak_neighbors() handles automatically.

title: Epidemiological Analysis with pkmapr
text: returned object contains: - w$nb: neighbours list - w$listw: row-standardised weights (ready spdep)

title: Epidemiological Analysis with pkmapr
text: significant p-value indicates spatial clustering.
code: set.seed(2023)  # Create spatially autocorrelated synthetic data n <- nrow(districts) rho <- 0.6 W_mat <- listw2mat(w$listw) epsilon <- rnorm(n, mean = 50, sd = 10) y <- as.numeric(solve(diag(n) - rho * W_mat) %*% epsilon)  districts$synthetic_rate <- y  # Test for spatial autocorrelation moran_result <- moran.test(districts$synthetic_rate, w$listw) print(moran_result)

title: Epidemiological Analysis with pkmapr
text: Identify local clusters outliers:
code: # Calculate local Moran's I lisa <- localmoran(districts$synthetic_rate, w$listw)  # Add results to districts districts$lisa_i <- lisa[, 1]  # local I districts$lisa_p <- lisa[, 5]  # p-value  # Classify clusters (simplified) districts$cluster <- case_when(   districts$lisa_p > 0.05 ~ "Not significant",   districts$synthetic_rate > mean(districts$synthetic_rate) &     districts$lisa_i > 0 ~ "High-High",   districts$synthetic_rate < mean(districts$synthetic_rate) &     districts$lisa_i > 0 ~ "Low-Low",   districts$lisa_i < 0 ~ "Outlier",   TRUE ~ "Other" )  # Map the clusters pak_map(districts, fill = "cluster", title = "Spatial Clusters")

title: Epidemiological Analysis with pkmapr
code: # Calculate Gi* for the synthetic data gi_star <- localG(districts$synthetic_rate, w$listw)  districts$gi_star <- as.numeric(gi_star) districts$hotspot <- ifelse(districts$gi_star > 1.96, "Hotspot",                             ifelse(districts$gi_star < -1.96, "Coldspot", "Not significant"))  pak_map(districts, fill = "hotspot", title = "Hotspots (p < 0.05)")

title: Epidemiological Analysis with pkmapr
text: Line Control creates analytical ambiguity. pak_neighbors() makes decision explicit:
code: # Exclude (default) — GB/AJK get nearest neighbour fallback w_exclude <- pak_neighbors(districts, disputed = "exclude")  # Include — treat disputed boundaries as shared w_include <- pak_neighbors(districts, disputed = "include")  # Flag — document which units are affected w_flag <- pak_neighbors(districts, disputed = "flag") print(w_flag$boundary_note)

title: Epidemiological Analysis with pkmapr
code: # 1. Get data districts <- get_districts()  # 2. Build weights with explicit dispute handling w <- pak_neighbors(districts, disputed = "exclude")  # 3. Attach your real case data (replace with your own) # districts <- pak_join(districts, your_case_data, by = "district_code")  # 4. Calculate rates (example) # districts <- districts |> #   mutate(rate = cases / population * 100000)  # 5. Test for spatial autocorrelation # moran.test(districts$rate, w$listw)  # 6. Identify clusters # lisa <- localmoran(districts$rate, w$listw) # districts$lisa_cluster <- attr(lisa, "quadr")$mean  # 7. Map results # pak_map(districts, fill = "lisa_cluster")

title: Epidemiological Analysis with pkmapr
text: Bivand, R. S., Pebesma, E., & Gomez-Rubio, V. (2013). Applied spatial data analysis R. Springer. OCHA Pakistan. (2023). COD-AB Administrative Boundaries.

title: intro-to-pkmapr
text: Install pkmapr GitHub: R-Universe ready:

title: intro-to-pkmapr
text: Get province boundaries create quick map:

title: intro-to-pkmapr
text: Always check official names filtering joining:
code: # All provinces with their codes pak_dictionary("provinces")  # Districts in Punjab pak_dictionary("districts", province = "Punjab")  # Tehsils in Lahore district pak_dictionary("tehsils", district = "Lahore")

title: intro-to-pkmapr
code: library(dplyr)  # Example: district-level data my_data <- data.frame(   district_code = c("PK603", "PK604"),   value = c(42, 37) )  districts <- get_districts() |>   pak_join(my_data, by = "district_code")  # Map the result pak_map(districts, fill = "value", title = "My Values")

title: intro-to-pkmapr
intro-to-pkmapr
text: vignette("spatial-analysis-pkmapr") buffers, centroids, point--polygon vignette("epidemiology-pkmapr") spatial autocorrelation hotspots

title: Spatial Analysis with pkmapr
text: Convert polygons points labeling distance calculations:
code: districts <- get_districts() centroids <- pak_centroid(districts)  # Map districts with centroid points pak_map(districts) +   ggplot2::geom_sf(data = centroids, color = "red", size = 0.5)

title: Spatial Analysis with pkmapr
text: Create buffer zones around administrative units (distances km):
code: # 10km buffer around Lahore district lahore <- get_districts(province = "Punjab") |>   filter(district_name == "Lahore")  lahore_buffer <- pak_buffer(lahore, dist_km = 10)  pak_map(lahore_buffer, fill = NA, color = "blue") +   ggplot2::geom_sf(data = lahore, fill = "red", alpha = 0.5)

title: Spatial Analysis with pkmapr
text: Compute distances units:
code: # Distance matrix between provinces (centroid to centroid) provinces <- get_provinces() dist_matrix <- pak_distance(provinces, provinces)  # Distance from each province to Karachi karachi <- get_districts(province = "Sindh") |>   filter(district_name == "Karachi")  distances <- pak_distance(provinces, karachi)

title: Spatial Analysis with pkmapr
text: Assign GPS points administrative units:
code: # Example: health facility locations facilities <- data.frame(   name = c("Hospital A", "Clinic B"),   lon = c(74.3, 74.5),   lat = c(31.5, 31.6) ) |>   sf::st_as_sf(coords = c("lon", "lat"), crs = 4326)  # Assign to districts facilities_with_district <- pak_points_in(facilities, districts)  # View result facilities_with_district |>   sf::st_drop_geometry() |>   select(name, district_name)

title: Spatial Analysis with pkmapr
text: Aggregate finer units coarser levels:
code: # Dissolve tehsils to district level tehsils <- get_tehsils() districts_from_tehsils <- pak_union(tehsils, by = "district_name")  # Compare area original_districts <- get_districts() pak_area(original_districts) pak_area(districts_from_tehsils)  # Should be similar

title: Spatial Analysis with pkmapr
text: WGS84 (default) measures degrees — use projected CRS real measurements:
code: # Recommended CRS for your data's extent pak_crs_suggest(get_districts(province = "Punjab"))  # Reproject for metric operations districts_utm <- pak_project(districts, crs = 32642)

title: Pakistan Administrative Boundaries Toolkit
text: pkmapr provides tidy interface Pakistan’s official administrative boundary data OCHA/HDX. Download province, district, tehsil geometries sf objects, look official names codes, prepare spatial data structures use spdep, ggplot2, leaflet.

what: Suggest an appropriate projected CRS for a Pakistan sf object — pak_crs_suggest
title: Suggest an appropriate projected CRS for a Pakistan sf object — pak_crs_suggest
text: Examines geographic extent sf object recommends appropriate projected coordinate reference system metric operations. Pakistan spans UTM zones 41N, 42N, 43N; national-level analyses benefit equal-area projection.

title: Suggest an appropriate projected CRS for a Pakistan sf object — pak_crs_suggest
Suggest an appropriate projected CRS for a Pakistan sf object — pak_crs_suggest
text: x sf object.

title: Suggest an appropriate projected CRS for a Pakistan sf object — pak_crs_suggest
text: named list: epsg Integer EPSG code recommended CRS. name Human-readable CRS name. rationale One-sentence explanation recommendation.

title: Suggest an appropriate projected CRS for a Pakistan sf object — pak_crs_suggest
text: Using WGS84 (EPSG:4326) distance, area, buffer operations produces inaccurate results measures degrees rather metres.

title: Suggest an appropriate projected CRS for a Pakistan sf object — pak_crs_suggest
code: # \donttest{   pak_crs_suggest(get_country()) #> $epsg #> [1] 102025 #>  #> $name #> [1] "Asia South Albers Equal Area Conic (ESRI:102025)" #>  #> $rationale #> [1] "Extent covers a large portion of Pakistan; Albers Equal Area Conic is recommended by OCHA for national-level Pakistan analyses and minimises area distortion across the full country." #>    pak_crs_suggest(get_districts(province = "Balochistan")) #> $epsg #> [1] 102025 #>  #> $name #> [1] "Asia South Albers Equal Area Conic (ESRI:102025)" #>  #> $rationale #> [1] "Extent covers a large portion of Pakistan; Albers Equal Area Conic is recommended by OCHA for national-level Pakistan analyses and minimises area distortion across the full country." #>  # }

NULL returns . district Character. Filter specific district. Applies level "tehsils". NULL returns . refresh Logical. Force re-download. Default FALSE.

ℹ 26 more rows   pak_dictionary("tehsils", district = "Lahore") #> # A tibble: 2 × 4 #>   province_name district_name tehsil_name  tehsil_code #>   <chr>         <chr>         <chr>        <chr>       #> 1 Punjab        Lahore        Lahore Cantt PK61701     #> 2 Punjab        Lahore        Lahore City  PK61702       pak_dictionary("tehsils", province = "Sindh") #> # A tibble: 125 × 4 #>    province_name district_name   tehsil_name          tehsil_code #>    <chr>         <chr>           <chr>                <chr>       #>  1 Sindh         Badin           Badin                PK70101     #>  2 Sindh         Badin           Matli                PK70102     #>  3 Sindh         Badin           Shaheed Fazil Rahu   PK70103     #>  4 Sindh         Badin           Talhar               PK70104     #>  5 Sindh         Badin           Tando Bago           PK70105     #>  6 Sindh         Central Karachi Gulberg Town         PK70201     #>  7 Sindh         Central Karachi Liaqatabad Town      PK70202     #>  8 Sindh         Central Karachi New Karachi Town     PK70203     #>  9 Sindh         Central Karachi North Nazimabad Town PK70204     #> 10 Sindh         Dadu            Dadu                 PK70301     #> # ℹ 115 more rows # }

what: Construct a spatial neighbours list for Pakistan administrative units — pak_neighbors
title: Construct a spatial neighbours list for Pakistan administrative units — pak_neighbors
text: Builds contiguity distance-based spatial neighbours structure direct use spdep spatialreg. Handles Pakistan-specific complexities including non-contiguous units disputed boundaries.

title: Construct a spatial neighbours list for Pakistan administrative units — pak_neighbors
code: pak_neighbors(   x,   style = c("queen", "rook", "knn"),   k = NULL,   disputed = c("exclude", "include", "flag") )

title: Construct a spatial neighbours list for Pakistan administrative units — pak_neighbors
text: x sf object polygon geometries. style Character. Neighbour definition: "queen" (shared boundary point, default), "rook" (shared edge), "knn" (k nearest centroids). k Integer. Number nearest neighbours. Required style = "knn". disputed Character. Treatment non-contiguous units disputed boundaries: exclude Default. Non-contiguous units receive one nearest neighbour fallback. informative message identifies affected units. include Treat boundaries normal shared boundaries. flag Include boundaries add boundary_note element result documenting units affected.

title: Construct a spatial neighbours list for Pakistan administrative units — pak_neighbors
text: named list: nb spdep nb object. listw row-standardised spdep listw object, ready spdep::moran.test(), spdep::localMoran(), spatialreg::lagsarlm(), related functions. boundary_note Character. Present disputed = "flag".

title: Construct a spatial neighbours list for Pakistan administrative units — pak_neighbors
text: Gilgit-Baltistan Azad Kashmir share land boundaries provinces receive zero neighbours standard contiguity rules, breaking spatial statistics. disputed argument controls Line Control special administrative boundaries flagged OCHA source data treated, making analytical decision explicit reproducible.

title: Construct a spatial neighbours list for Pakistan administrative units — pak_neighbors
code: # \donttest{   districts <- get_districts()   w <- pak_neighbors(districts)    # Pass directly to spdep   # spdep::moran.test(districts$area_km2, w$listw) # }

what: pkmapr: Access and Analyse Pakistan Administrative Boundary Data — pkmapr-package
title: pkmapr: Access and Analyse Pakistan Administrative Boundary Data — pkmapr-package
text: Provides tidy interface Pakistan's official administrative boundary data United Nations Office Coordination Humanitarian Affairs (OCHA). Downloads caches spatial data country, province, district, tehsil levels sf objects compatible tidyverse geospatial ecosystem. Includes utilities geographic dictionary lookup, coordinate reference system selection, spatial measurement, neighbour structure construction use spdep, ggplot2, leaflet, related packages.

title: pkmapr: Access and Analyse Pakistan Administrative Boundary Data — pkmapr-package
text: Maintainer: Abdullah Umer abdullahumer1101@gmail.com (ORCID)
